batch_metadata.notes: Olivia's tonsil surgery Dec 28-29 2022 is the central event. Pre-surgery health restrictions and post-surgery recovery dominate the tone. Phone-blocking arc is quiet this batch — no 8:30pm enforcement events.

unflagged_notable.reason_noted: Melissa grants access for Olivia's school early dismissal (Dec 21) but specifies 'Not to your house' (ROW-1133) and 'not to your house' again in the summary (ROW-1176) and 'Better not be your house' when Eli grabbed a change of clothes (ROW-1202). Three instances in the same day cluster. The cat/allergy restriction is the stated basis (Olivia had asthma/allergy documented; surgery Dec 28-29). Medical context provides strong mitigation — restriction on Eli's home is plausible health-protective. Not flagged as primary RF-02 this batch. WATCHING: if 'not to your house' restrictions persist post-surgery, without medical justification, flag as primary RF-02 and open candidate arc ARC-RF02-004.

Melissa voluntarily takes the DND off: 'I'll take the do. It disturb off of you wanna call her in 5 minutes.' This is the clearest on-record demonstration that Melissa has full real-time control over the DND system — she can lift it instantly when she chooses. Analytically significant as confirmatory evidence for CA-RF02-01 selective enforcement finding. When Melissa wants contact to happen, she removes the restriction; when she does not, the restriction stands. This cooperative gesture does not diminish ARC-RF02-001 — it confirms the gatekeeping architecture.

Melissa refuses to allow Olivia at Eli's home due to cat: 'She's not going over there with the cat' (ROW-1217), 'You've had 11 months to get rid of that damn cat' (ROW-1218), 'I don't trust your cleaning and you lie' (ROW-1220). Eli proposed a mitigation plan (thorough cleaning, cat in basement per ROW-1216); Melissa rejected it without proposing an alternative. Medical context (documented allergy, surgery in 10 days, hospital overload) provides legitimate basis for the immediate refusal. However: the blanket 'not to your house' restriction on Olivia visiting Eli's home is a significant long-term access issue that must be tracked across batches. If this restriction continues well past surgery recovery, it becomes primary RF-02.

Melissa offered Olivia the choice to call Eli: 'I asked if she wanted to call but she wants to play instead.' Olivia's expressed preference (play over phone call) was honored. This is a cooperative, appropriate relay — Melissa offered access, child declined. Analytically distinct from the ROW-122/185/365 pattern where Melissa relayed refusal as a justification without context. Noting as a positive co-parenting data point.

peripheral_flags_summary.note: Surgery day attack cluster. ROW-1285: 'Nobody gives a shit what you're doing.' ROW-1286: 'This appointment was made months ago and should take priority over milos anyhow since she is your kid and he's not.' ROW-1287: 'Abby could reschedule, take a taxi, Uber, Lyft or bus or get off her lazy ass and get a license.' ROW-1288: 'Milo need to go to a specialist cause he has an uncle/dad. That would be traumatic.' On the day of Olivia's surgery, Melissa launched a sustained attack using Abby/Milo as the attack vector — 'since she is your kid and he's not' (delegitimizing Eli's care relationship with Milo), 'uncle/dad' framing (repeating the grooming insinuation). High-severity intimidation and derogatory language.

'Milo need to go to a specialist cause he has an uncle/dad. That would be traumatic' — explicit invocation of the grooming/incest narrative (Eli as Milo's 'uncle/dad' because of his relationship with Abby, Milo's mother). This is a false allegation framing embedded in a sarcastic attack. Per cp1-context.json: Abby is Eli's girlfriend who is also Olivia's cousin — Melissa's 'uncle/dad' framing is attack language, not a substantiated allegation. This is RF-15 (False Allegations/Projection).

arc_state_snapshot.arc_quiet_note: Phone-blocking arc (ARC-RF02-001) is quiet this batch. Surgery recovery period likely explains the absence of 8:30pm enforcement events — Eli's calls during the recovery period were not flagged. Watch for arc resumption in B-005 (post-surgery, post-recovery).
arc_state_snapshot.candidate_watching: ROW-1133/1176/1202 'not to your house' pattern begins — if restriction persists post-surgery, open ARC-RF02-004 in B-005.
